subject: Project Management
title: The Stakeholder
description: A fast breakdown of The Stakeholder for builders.

hook.text: Stakeholders fund, use, or are affected by the product — but they're not on the Scrum team.

buildup.text: Their input flows through the Product Owner, who translates needs into backlog items.

discovery.text: Sprint Reviews are where stakeholders see progress and give feedback — their official touchpoint.

twist.text: Stakeholders who bypass the Product Owner and directly assign work to the team destroy Scrum.

climax.text: Scrum gives stakeholders visibility and voice — but not a steering wheel.

punchline.text: You get a voice. You don't get the wheel.

quiz.question: How do stakeholders provide input in Scrum?
options: Through the Product Owner and at Sprint Reviews
By directly assigning tasks to developers
